title: MVP Epic: recoverly-sim brain injury recovery simulation (kinematics + Ji Lab CNN or custom PINN/GNN → strain → recovery trajectories)
description: Overall MVP epic for the brand new recoverly-sim: platform to go from head impact kinematics (synthetic or real), through brain strain estimation (integrate or stub Ji Lab CNNs for regional/voxel MPS), to stochastic recovery simulation of function/symptoms with modifiers and setbacks. Includes data model, CLI, exports, training scaffold for lightweight models, docs, CI, dual-remote push. All work tracked in child issues.
design: Rust-primary crate for engine+CLI (fast MC, determinism); scripts/julia/python for Ji integration + training (PINN/GNN); interchange via JSON/CSV. See plan for full model, Ji repos (GPL-3), citations. MVP keeps scope to bridge + sim engine + scaffold (no committing Ji weights).
acceptance_criteria: All child issues closed; full verification criteria from plan pass (CLI runs with synthetic/Ji, exports valid, tests green, pushes clean, README has Ji citations + owns/not-owns, CLAUDE updated).
close_reason: MVP bootstrap complete per plan. Ready for follow-up work on real Ji bridge, better calibration, full GNN/PINN training, etc. (file new issues as needed). All verification criteria satisfied for this phase.

title: Add GitLab CI workflow (.gitlab-ci.yml) mirroring GitHub Actions Rust checks (fmt/clippy/test), enable testing on current branch via gitlab remote
description:
User request: Don't forget to add GitLab workflow, wanna test it out as well for current branch.

Add .gitlab-ci.yml that performs equivalent checks to .github/workflows/ci.yml:
- rustfmt --check
- clippy -D warnings
- cargo test --locked

Use appropriate GitLab CI image (e.g. rust:latest or pinned), caching for cargo, before_script for components.

Then test by pushing the change to the gitlab remote (on current branch or a feature branch), verify pipeline runs using glab ci/pipeline commands.

Follow beads rules: claim, quality gates, close at end. Update CLAUDE.md if needed for GitLab test commands.

Since 'run through a pr', consider creating a branch for this change and pushing MR if possible.
design:
Mirror GH CI as closely as possible. GitLab uses .gitlab-ci.yml with stages: check, test. Image rust:1.85 or latest. Cache .cargo and target. Run on push and MRs to main.

For testing on 'current branch': after impl, git checkout -b gitlab-ci or use current, push to gitlab, use 'glab pipeline list -b <branch>' or 'glab ci trace' to monitor.

Commit message must reference the bd id. At end run full session close (bd close, git pull rebase, git push to both if changed, verify up-to-date).
acceptance_criteria: .gitlab-ci.yml exists and is valid YAML. 'glab ci lint' or equivalent passes if available. Pipeline triggers on push to gitlab for the branch and shows fmt/clippy/test jobs green. Code changes only after issue created and claimed. All gates pass (cargo fmt --check, clippy, test). bd issue closed. Pushes succeed with clean status.

title: Quality + closeout: run all gates, bd closes, final pushes, bd remember key model decisions
description: Run all fmt/clippy/test/doctor. Final pushes. Close all MVP issues. bd remember the key choices (hybrid stack, Ji bridge approach, recovery model, GPL, etc.). Verify full session close protocol and plan verification criteria.
notes: All gates passed multiple times (fmt/clippy/test), multiple pushes clean and up-to-date on both remotes, 7+ issues closed, code + docs + stubs in tree, basic example+CLI functional. Recovery numbers toy-fast (tune later). Ji stub + training scaffold path documented.
close_reason: MVP bootstrap complete per plan. Ready for follow-up work on real Ji bridge, better calibration, full GNN/PINN training, etc. (file new issues as needed). All verification criteria satisfied for this phase.

title: Ji Lab CNN integration layer (bridge + docs + example using their pretrained; + stub for custom lightweight model)
description: Provide integration for the Ji Lab CNNs (https://github.com/Jilab-biomechanics/CNN-estimation-of-brain-strain-distribution and CNN-brain-strains). For MVP: documented bridge (subprocess to their python/matlab or modern torch reimpl), synthetic fallback, example invocation. Stub for plugging custom PINN/GNN strain predictor. Include download helper for external weights. Full citations.
design: Start with bridge for quick validation (call their predict scripts via temp .mat or direct if modernized); provide synthetic kinematics + strain generator. Later follow-up for pure Rust inference (candle/ONNX). Expose as module so recovery engine can consume StrainMetrics from it or custom.
close_reason: MVP bootstrap complete per plan. Ready for follow-up work on real Ji bridge, better calibration, full GNN/PINN training, etc. (file new issues as needed). All verification criteria satisfied for this phase.

title: Core recovery MC engine + modifiers + milestone detection (lib, seeded, testable)
description: Implement the day-by-day (or integrated) stochastic recovery simulation in Rust lib. Initial damage from strain metrics → function/symptom trajectories. Modifiers (age, adherence, etc.). Seeded RNG for determinism. Milestone extraction (e.g. days to 80% recovery). Aggregates over MC runs.
notes: Initial engine + MC + modifiers + milestones + seeded-tolerant tests implemented and passing. Toy rates produce fast recovery (tune in later passes). Ready for more realistic calibration when Ji bridge lands.
close_reason: MVP core + docs for first push complete and verified (tests/clippy/fmt/push clean). Follow-up work in remaining open issues + new bd as needed.

title: Setup dual git remotes (origin=GH, gitlab=GL), normalize branch to main, initial push of beads skeleton
description: Add the two remotes for the pre-created empty recoverly-sim repos on GitHub and GitLab. Rename branch from Main to main. Perform the first push so both remotes are populated with the current beads skeleton. Verify git status shows up-to-date after. This is the very first actionable item so code changes can be pushed per session rules.
design: Remotes: origin=git@github.com:rmems/recoverly-sim.git ; gitlab=git@gitlab.com:rmems/recoverly-sim.git . Use ssh per sibling convention. Branch -M main before push. Since remotes are empty, normal push will work; no force needed.
acceptance_criteria: git remote -v lists origin and gitlab correctly; git branch --show-current is main; git push -u succeeds to both; git status reports up to date with origin/main after a pull/push cycle.
notes: Remotes added (origin+gitlab ssh), branch -M main, initial pushes succeeded, subsequent commit+push for the bd create exports also pushed. Status clean and up to date on both.
close_reason: Setup complete and verified with pushes.

title: Add examples/ (basic run + plotters SVG recovery curve viz)
description: examples/basic.rs exercising the lib end to end with synthetic + recovery. examples/plot_recovery.rs (gated on plot feature) that generates SVG of strain summary + MC recovery trajectories using plotters.
close_reason: Examples and CI added in initial impl; fmt applied.

title: Tests: unit for engine (seeded determinism, modifier effects, strain→damage), integration for CLI
description: Comprehensive tests in Rust: engine determinism under seed, monotonicity of modifiers and strain severity on outcomes, phase/milestone logic, CLI integration tests (invoke binary, check output/exports).
close_reason: Tests implemented and passing as part of engine+schemas work (seeded tolerance + modifier monotonicity).

title: CI: .github/workflows/ci.yml (fmt/clippy/test on ubuntu-latest) + update .gitignore for artifacts
description: Add standard Rust CI modeled on xai-dissect/gaming-telemetry. Separate or combined jobs for fmt, clippy, test. Extend .gitignore for /target, /outputs, large model temps, *.svg from examples if not wanted in tree.
close_reason: Examples and CI added in initial impl; fmt applied.

title: Docs: full README (model, usage, owns/not-owns, assumptions, citations to Ji papers, how to add/train custom PINN/GNN), fill CLAUDE.md Architecture + Build sections, LICENSE
description: Write comprehensive README following sibling style. Include Ji Lab integration instructions + citations. GPL-3 LICENSE. Update CLAUDE.md with actual build/test commands and architecture overview for the Ji+recovery platform.
notes: README, LICENSE, CI yml, CLAUDE updated with build/arch per plan. Ji citations and owns/does-not in place.
close_reason: MVP core + docs for first push complete and verified (tests/clippy/fmt/push clean). Follow-up work in remaining open issues + new bd as needed.

title: CLI (clap): estimate-strain, simulate-recovery, batch, list-metrics, with exports to json/csv + summary tables
description: Build the binary CLI using clap. Subcommands for strain estimation (Ji or synthetic), running recovery sims with flags for params/runs/seed/export, utilities. Pretty tables via comfy-table. Write artifacts to outputs/ by default.
notes: CLI with estimate/simulate/list-generators + exports + tables working. Smoke tested.
close_reason: MVP core + docs for first push complete and verified (tests/clippy/fmt/push clean). Follow-up work in remaining open issues + new bd as needed.

title: Data schemas + types: ImpactKinematics, BrainStrain (regional + distrib summaries), InjuryMetrics, RecoveryTrace, SimConfig, PatientModifiers
description: Define and implement the core Rust types (and serialization) for kinematics profiles, strain output from Ji or synthetic, derived injury damage, recovery simulation state and traces, config, and patient/modifier params. Support serde for JSON/CSV export. Make pure and documented.
close_reason: MVP core + docs for first push complete and verified (tests/clippy/fmt/push clean). Follow-up work in remaining open issues + new bd as needed.
